meta.note: Algeria + Sudan + Ethiopia sweep, published books. Deduped against existing DZ/SD/ET records. Love in the Time of Cholera already existed -> Sudan ban-add via existing_book_id. Christ Re-crucified = The Greek Passion (one Kazantzakis book). Held low-confidence: DZ L'Insolation (paywalled source), ET two Be'alu Girma titles (blanket-ban inference only). Sudan record is almost entirely Khartoum International Book Fair confiscations reported by Radio Dabanga; bans are historical (Bashir-era). Algeria via SILA bans + Sansal/Benchicou/Boudjedra cases.

entries.title: Houris
entries.description_ban: Goncourt-winning novel about a survivor of a 'black decade' massacre; barred from the 2024 Algiers International Book Fair as contravening the Charter for Peace and National Reconciliation, which forbids evoking the civil-war 'national tragedy'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; reported by Euronews and France 24.

entries.title: Poste restante: Alger
entries.description_ban: Open-letter essay attacking the Algerian regime; its publication triggered the standing ban on Sansal's books in Algeria.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban (trigger of the Sansal ban); documented via Wikipedia citing Haaretz.

entries.title: Le village de l'Allemand
entries.description_ban: Novel drawing parallels between Nazism and Islamism; covered by the blanket ban on Sansal's works in Algeria since 2006.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title named under the Sansal ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: 2084: la fin du monde
entries.description_ban: Dystopia of an Islamist totalitarian state (Académie française grand prix); covered by the standing ban on Sansal's works in Algeria.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title named under the Sansal ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: Bouteflika: Une imposture algérienne
entries.description_ban: Critical biography of President Bouteflika; its release led to the closure of Benchicou's paper Le Matin and his imprisonment, and the book's circulation was suppressed.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented by The Arab Weekly and Wikipedia.

entries.title: Journal d'un homme libre
entries.description_ban: A police order blocked the book and barred Benchicou from presenting it at the 13th Algiers International Book Fair (October 2008).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair ban; documented by IFEX.

entries.title: Les Geôles d'Alger
entries.description_ban: Memoir of Benchicou's imprisonment; police halted its production and blocked it from the Algiers book fair the year before the Journal incident.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair ban; documented by IFEX.

entries.title: La Répudiation
entries.description_ban: Boudjedra's first novel, attacking Muslim traditionalism and Algerian taboos; banned in Algeria until around 1980 and occasioning a fatwa against the author.

entries.title: The Messiah of Darfur
entries.description_ban: Novel on the Darfur conflict; seized by security at the February 2017 Khartoum book fair, after Sakin's books were already confiscated at the 2012 fair, forcing his exile.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; documented by Radio Dabanga and FairPlanet.

entries.title: Holy Chains
entries.description_ban: Confiscated at the 2016 Khartoum book fair with a ban on its circulation, display and sale.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair ban; documented by Radio Dabanga.

entries.title: Christ Re-crucified
entries.description_ban: Kazantzakis's novel (also 'The Greek Passion') confiscated at the February 2017 Khartoum book fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; documented by Radio Dabanga.

entries.title: Bird of Doom
entries.description_ban: Work by the South Sudanese scholar-writer confiscated at the February 2017 Khartoum book fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; documented by Radio Dabanga (medium confidence).

entries.title: Their Story is My Story
entries.description_ban: Subtitled 'Women and Religion'; ordered withdrawn from the 2017 Khartoum book fair 'until it has been reviewed'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair withdrawal; documented by Radio Dabanga (medium confidence).

entries.title: The Bar: The Story of Cracks and Slaves
entries.description_ban: Named among books pulled at the 2017 Khartoum book fair; no official reason was given.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair ban; documented by Radio Dabanga (medium confidence).

entries.title: Two Skulls for the Sun
entries.description_ban: Named among books banned at the 2017 Khartoum book fair; no official reason was given.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair ban; documented by Radio Dabanga (medium confidence).

entries.title: Love in the Time of Cholera
entries.description_ban: Circulation, display and sale banned by Sudanese security at the 2016 Khartoum book fair. Adds a Sudan ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Sudan ban; documented by Radio Dabanga.

entries.title: Fabula: Yawreoch Commedia
entries.description_ban: Animal-fable play satirising imperial court corruption; after ~300 of 3,000 copies sold, Empress Zewditu ordered all copies collected and banned theatre — Ethiopia's earliest documented title ban.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban with copies confiscated; documented by The File Room (NCAC) and African Theatre Docs.

entries.title: Alweledim
entries.description_ban: Allegorical novel ('I Will Not Be Born') set in a fictional kingdom; banned and burnt after ~800 copies sold, and the author imprisoned three years under Haile Selassie.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban and burning; documented via Wikipedia with citations.

entries.title: The Tragic Death of Patrice Lumumba
entries.description_ban: Play heavily censored under the Haile Selassie regime; part of the pattern that led to Gubegna's repeated detention.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific censorship; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).
